features.summary: Caterpillar reported Q2 2022 adjusted EPS of $3.18 versus $2.60 prior year, with sales increasing 11% to $14.2 billion driven by strong pricing and services revenue. Operating profit grew 9% to $1.9 billion despite supply chain constraints limiting equipment deliveries to end users. The company generated $1.1 billion in ME&T free cash flow and returned $1.7 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. Backlog increased $2 billion in the quarter, led by Energy & Transportation, while dealer inventories remain at the low end of typical ranges.
features.management_outlook: Management expects continued top-line growth in the second half with sequential and year-over-year improvements in both volume and pricing. They anticipate adjusted operating profit margins will improve in H2 as price realization accelerates and more than offsets manufacturing cost increases. The company reaffirmed its Investor Day targets for adjusted operating profit margin and ME&T free cash flow of $4-8 billion for full year 2022. Infrastructure spending from the U.S. Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act is expected to provide support in late 2022 and into 2023.
features.risks: Supply chain constraints continue to be the primary challenge, particularly semiconductor shortages affecting engine control modules, limiting the company's ability to meet strong customer demand. Manufacturing inefficiencies and elevated freight costs persist due to supply disruptions. Inflationary pressures on materials and freight remain elevated, though price increases are expected to more than offset these costs. Geopolitical tensions resulted in some backlog cancellations in Russia, and China's construction market has weakened more than previously expected.
features.key_drivers: Strong demand across most end markets with healthy order levels and elevated backlogs supporting future growth. Services revenue momentum continues with 1.2 million connected assets and investments in digital capabilities and parts availability driving results toward the goal of doubling services to $28 billion by 2026. Pricing actions are gaining traction and expected to accelerate in the second half, with price realization already exceeding manufacturing cost increases in Q2. Mining customers remain disciplined but supportive of long-term investment driven by energy transition commodity demand.
